microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user likely wants food served warm without excessive heat. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE fatigue makes repeated reheating inconvenient and warmer food may stay pleasant longer. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 1 min BECAUSE speed matters more than maximizing warmth. IF social setting AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE this balances comfort and predictable serving temperature. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND refrigerated savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE distraction may slow eating, so slightly hotter food stays warm longer. IF any context AND neutral AND fruit or cold dessert THEN no microwave BECAUSE those foods are typically preferred cold. IF social setting AND relaxed AND hot-sensitive meal like brownie or soft dessert THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user may prefer avoiding overly hot bites while conversing.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE efficiency matters more than view management in a quiet personal setting. IF watching TV AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user likely wants to keep the screen visible. IF working on laptop or tablet AND neutral AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the display is directly in front and task continuity matters. IF social setting AND partner on left AND any affect AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE preserving sightline to the partner is important. IF social setting AND partner on right AND any affect AND any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE preserving sightline to the partner is important. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE social visibility still takes priority over fatigue for this user when another person is present. IF watching TV or working AND in a hurry AND any food THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this user consistently prioritizes access to the attended screen over small efficiency gains.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE the user has limited neck and trunk control and benefits from predictable motion. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND easy food like fruit pieces or brownie THEN medium BECAUSE the user accepts some speed increase when efficiency matters and food is simple. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE comfort and safety outweigh speed when energy is low. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE slower motion is less stressful and more socially comfortable. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN slow BECAUSE distraction increases the value of predictable movement. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND easy food THEN medium BECAUSE urgency can raise speed slightly, but social comfort prevents fast motion. IF any context AND difficult food like slippery sauced items or awkward meats THEN slow BECAUSE careful motion reduces transfer and acquisition errors.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND elongated or firm food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE narrower bites are easier given slow mouth opening. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND bite acquisition has been failing on firm food THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE reliability temporarily outweighs ideal bite shape. IF social setting AND neutral AND elongated food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user prefers easier-to-eat bites over maximizing acquisition success. IF social setting AND in a hurry AND repeated failures on meat or fruit THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE avoiding repeated failed attempts is more important in front of others. IF fatigued AND any context AND elongated food THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE reduced effort during mouth acceptance is the tie-break priority. IF any context AND soft dessert or irregular food that already forms a small bite THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE bite width is less concerning and acquisition success improves. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND difficult skewering food THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE reducing retries helps when attention is divided.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user can access the web interface and values avoiding empty-fork transfers. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND easy food with reliable acquisition THEN no BECAUSE efficiency can outweigh explicit confirmation when risk is low. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE assistive web interaction becomes more effortful when tired. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE avoiding visible failed transfers matters in front of others. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE this user's tie-break policy favors reducing user effort under fatigue even in social contexts. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE extra confirmation interrupts the primary activity. IF any context AND difficult food like slippery, crumbly, or awkward items THEN yes BECAUSE the chance of failed bite acquisition is higher.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE physical limitations make outside-mouth transfer impractical despite possible social discomfort. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE divided attention makes self-initiated reach-to-bite even less feasible. IF fatigued AND any context AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it minimizes required body movement. IF in a hurry AND any context AND any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE it is the most direct transfer method for this user. IF any context AND bulky or awkward bite THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE precise delivery reduces the need for user repositioning. IF relaxed AND social setting AND easy food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user's motor constraints consistently override contextual preferences.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE the default transfer mode is inside mouth transfer. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user still cannot lean forward reliably. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer would demand extra self-positioning. IF fatigued AND any context AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE fatigue further reduces ability to reach the bite. IF in a hurry AND any context AND any food THEN not applicable BECAUSE inside-mouth transfer remains the only practical option. IF any context AND easy small bite THEN not applicable BECAUSE food simplicity does not change the user's trunk-control limitation. IF any context AND social pressure to keep more distance from the mouth THEN not applicable BECAUSE physical feasibility overrides distancing preferences.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE multimodal cues are clear without requiring extra effort. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food THEN speech BECAUSE the user may want the fastest, most direct cue. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues reduce missed signals when attention is low. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE quieter signaling is less disruptive around others. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE this user's fatigue tie-break favors cue clarity over social subtlety. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN LED BECAUSE visual cues are less interruptive to ongoing media or work. IF watching TV or working AND fatigued AND any food THEN speech + LED BECAUSE distraction plus fatigue makes redundancy more valuable than minimizing interruption.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and can still signal readiness orally. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue makes even slow mouth-opening less desirable and the user tolerates more automation. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE reducing interaction steps speeds the meal. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN open mouth BECAUSE button use is not feasible and the user still wants some explicit control. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE this user's tie-break policy prioritizes minimizing effort under fatigue over avoiding social ambiguity. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN autocontinue BECAUSE attention is divided and automatic progression is easier. IF social setting AND relaxed AND conversation-heavy meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE when not fatigued the user prefers retaining intentional initiation despite possible confusion with talking.

IF eating alone AND neutral AND any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE it provides feasible user-driven initiation without button use. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user prefers lower-effort interaction when tired. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE automatic starts reduce delays. IF social setting AND neutral AND any drink THEN open mouth BECAUSE some explicit control is preferred when not fatigued. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE minimizing effort consistently wins under fatigue. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE divided attention makes manual-style initiation less practical. IF social setting AND relaxed AND conversation-heavy meal with frequent speaking THEN open mouth BECAUSE despite some ambiguity, it remains more feasible than button input and preserves control.

IF eating alone AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN open mouth BECAUSE it is a feasible explicit cue without requiring hand use. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user prefers the robot to proceed with less effort. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE wiping is secondary and the user wants fewer pauses. IF social setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user wants some control over a more personal action. IF social setting AND fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue remains the tie-break priority even for intimate tasks. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE interruption cost is high during another activity. IF social setting AND relaxed AND messy food caused frequent wiping THEN open mouth BECAUSE when energy is adequate the user prefers intentional timing for wiping.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN speech + LED BECAUSE redundant cues clearly indicate when to take the bite or sip. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food or drink THEN speech BECAUSE the user may prefer a direct cue with minimal delay. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any food or drink THEN speech + LED BECAUSE extra cue clarity helps when response timing is slower. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN LED BECAUSE quieter signaling is more socially discreet. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food or drink THEN speech + LED BECAUSE fatigue makes missed cues more costly than social subtlety. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food or drink THEN LED BECAUSE it is less disruptive to the attended task. IF watching TV or working AND fatigued AND any food or drink THEN speech + LED BECAUSE distraction plus fatigue favors stronger cueing.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN perception BECAUSE it is reliable and avoids impossible button use. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any food THEN perception BECAUSE it minimizes effort while remaining accurate. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food THEN perception BECAUSE it is faster than waiting for timeout. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN perception BECAUSE it avoids awkward extra signaling in front of others. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN perception BECAUSE the user still benefits from low-effort reliable detection. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN perception BECAUSE divided attention makes explicit signaling undesirable. IF any context AND unusually soft or hard bite texture THEN perception BECAUSE force-based completion remains more dependable than autocontinue for feeding.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably nod or press a button. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it minimizes effort and accommodates reduced control. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE automatic completion speeds the interaction. IF social setting AND neutral AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE head-gesture perception is not feasible and button use is unavailable. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE fatigue further strengthens the low-effort preference. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user may not want to interrupt the primary activity. IF any context AND cautious sipping of hot drink THEN autocontinue BECAUSE although slower, it remains the only compatible completion method.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably use button or head-gesture completion. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it minimizes effort during a secondary task. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE it avoids extra signaling steps. IF social setting AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE explicit completion methods are not physically practical. IF social setting AND fatigued AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE low-effort automation remains the consistent tie-break. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND mouth wiping needed THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user is likely distracted and benefits from automatic progression. IF any context AND messy sauce requires repeated wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE repeated explicit completion would be burdensome and infeasible.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN no BECAUSE the user prioritizes efficiency when no external view target must be preserved. IF watching TV AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE retracting helps keep the screen visible. IF working on laptop or tablet AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE visual access to the device matters. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN yes BECAUSE prolonged obstruction can interfere with interaction with the other person. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN no BECAUSE this user's tie-break policy favors reducing effort and meal duration under fatigue. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food THEN no BECAUSE skipping retraction shortens the meal. IF any context AND occlusion target is important but the user is also in a hurry THEN yes BECAUSE this user prioritizes maintaining view access whenever a clear visual target is present.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND no sauce available THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is irrelevant without a dip. IF eating alone AND neutral AND sauce available THEN less BECAUSE the user may enjoy flavor but still prefers manageable, low-mess bites. IF eating alone AND relaxed AND sauce available THEN more BECAUSE with ample time the user is more open to richer bites. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND sauce available THEN do not dip BECAUSE skipping dipping reduces steps and mess. IF social setting AND neutral AND sauce available THEN less BECAUSE the user wants to avoid messy transfers in front of others. IF social setting AND fatigued AND sauce available THEN do not dip BECAUSE fatigue plus mess risk makes dipping not worth it. IF watching TV or working AND sauce available THEN do not dip BECAUSE minimizing mess and distraction is the priority.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF eating alone AND neutral AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user may need extra time to respond but does not want extremely long pauses. IF eating alone AND fatigued AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE slower mouth opening and reduced energy make a long window safer. IF eating alone AND in a hurry AND any food THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user accepts more automation to finish faster. IF social setting AND neutral AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user may be intermittently distracted by conversation. IF social setting AND fatigued AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE fatigue plus social distraction strongly favors a long intervention window. IF watching TV or working AND neutral AND any food THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE attention may be divided and the user needs more time to notice and respond. IF watching TV or working AND in a hurry AND any food THEN 100 sec BECAUSE urgency shortens the wait, but not to the minimum when attention is still split.
